title: carex simpliciuscula subsp. subholarctica
intro: Carex simpliciuscula subsp. subholarctica, also known as northern dwarf sedge, is a subspecies of sedge in the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Carex simpliciuscula subsp. subholarctica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be carefully evaluated due to limited research on its safety and efficacy.
